title: Bookstore

shortDialogue.speaker: 손님
shortDialogue.korean: 이 책 언제 살 수 있어요?
shortDialogue.english: When can I buy this book?

longDialogue.speaker: 손님
longDialogue.korean: 저기요. 이 책을 찾고 있는데 못 찾겠어요.
longDialogue.english: Excuse me. I'm looking for this book, but I can't find it.

longDialogue.speaker: 점원
longDialogue.korean: 이 책은 지금 없어요. 이 책이 인기가 많아서 다 팔렸어요.
longDialogue.english: We don't have this book now. This book is popular, so it's sold out.

longDialogue.speaker: 손님
longDialogue.english: Then, when can I buy it?

longDialogue.speaker: 점원
longDialogue.korean: 아직 모르겠어요. 다른 서점에 있을 수도 있어요.
longDialogue.english: I don't know yet. Other bookstores might have it.

longDialogue.speaker: 손님
longDialogue.korean: 다른 서점은 못 가요. 여기에서 사야 돼요.
longDialogue.english: I can't go to other bookstores. I have to buy it here.

longDialogue.speaker: 손님
longDialogue.english: Until when do I have to wait?

longDialogue.speaker: 손님
longDialogue.korean: 다음 주에는 책을 살 수 있어요?
longDialogue.english: Can I buy the book next week?

culturalTip: Online bookstores in Korea, which usually offered big discounts on new books, were growing in popularity until the government introduced a new "book price control system" in 2014. The new system restricts the discount rate of new books to a maximum of 10 percent of the cover price. As a result, more people started to buy secondhand books. To meet the increase in demand, online booksellers have begun opening up secondhand bookstores in busy areas in Korea.
koreanReview:
- Short Dialogue
손님: 이 책 언제 살 수 있어요?
점원: 다음 주에 다시 오세요.

- Long Dialogue
손님: 저기요. 이 책을 찾고 있는데 못 찾겠어요.
점원: 이 책은 지금 없어요. 이 책이 인기가 많아서 다 팔렸어요.
손님: 그럼 언제 살 수 있어요?
점원: 아직 모르겠어요. 다른 서점에 있을 수도 있어요.
손님: 다른 서점은 못 가요. 여기에서 사야 돼요.
점원: 그럼 기다려야 돼요.
손님: 언제까지 기다려야 해요?
점원: 다음 주에 다시 오세요.
손님: 다음 주에는 책을 살 수 있어요?
점원: 그럴 것 같아요.
손님: 확실하지 않아요?
점원: 네. 확실하지 않아요.

extendedVocab.meaning: to not exist, to not have, to not be there

grammar.title: Cannot Do Something
grammar.explanation: Place 못 before the verb to express that one cannot do the action.

grammar.title: Conjunction: "but"
grammar.explanation: Attach -(으)ㄴ/는데 to a verb or adjective to connect clauses with the meaning of "but".

examples.korean: 찾고 있다 + -(으)ㄴ/는데 + 못 찾겠어요 → 찾고 있는데 못 찾겠어요.
examples.english: I'm looking for (it), but I can't find it.

exercises.english: It's not popular, but it's sold out.
I'm hungry, but there's no food.

pronunciation.rule: When a syllable ends with ㅆ (which sounds the same as ㄷ when in the 받침 position) and is followed by a syllable that begins with ㄴ, the ㅆ is pronounced as ㄴ.
When a syllable ends with ㅅ (which sounds the same as ㄷ when in the 받침 position) and is followed by a syllable that begins with ㄱ, the ㄱ is pronounced as ㄲ.
